benefits.benefit_desc: Dan Shen, also known as Salvia miltiorrhiza, improves blood circulation, meaning it helps to promote the flow of blood in the body, which can lead to better distribution of oxygen and nutrients to the body's tissues. The main active constituents of Salvia miltiorrhiza that give this benefit are tanshinones and salvianolic acids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and vasodilatory effects. The primary parts of the Salvia miltiorrhiza plant that give this benefit are the roots, which contain the highest concentration of the active compounds. The main medicinal preparations of Salvia miltiorrhiza that give this benefit include decoctions, powdered extracts, and capsules. The main health conditions that this benefit of Salvia miltiorrhiza helps relieve include peripheral arterial disease, angina, and stroke. Additionally, it is also used to improve blood flow in the body, which can help with various health conditions such as chronic wounds, erectile dysfunction and etc.

Dan Shen, also known as Salvia miltiorrhiza, reduces blood stasis, meaning it helps to improve the flow of blood and reduce blood clots in the body. The main active constituents of Salvia miltiorrhiza that contribute to this benefit are tanshinones and salvianolic acids. The root of the Salvia miltiorrhiza plant is the primary part used for medicinal purposes to reduce blood stasis. The main medicinal preparations of Salvia miltiorrhiza that provide this benefit are decoctions, powder, and ethanol or water extracts. The main health conditions that this benefit of Salvia miltiorrhiza helps relieve include chronic inflammation, cardiovascular diseases, and cerebrovascular diseases.

Dan Shen, also known as Salvia miltiorrhiza, alleviates chest pain, meaning it can help reduce the discomfort and pain associated with angina or other heart-related conditions. The main active constituents of Salvia miltiorrhiza that contribute to this benefit are tanshinones and salvianolic acids, which have been shown to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation. The primary parts of the Salvia miltiorrhiza plant that provide this benefit are the roots and rhizomes, which contain the highest concentration of these active compounds. The main medicinal preparations of Salvia miltiorrhiza that provide this benefit include decoctions, powdered extracts, and capsules. This benefit of Salvia miltiorrhiza is particularly helpful for those with coronary artery disease, angina, and other heart conditions that cause chest pain.

Dan Shen, also known as Salvia miltiorrhiza, regulates the menstrual cycle, meaning it can help to balance hormones and alleviate menstrual-related symptoms, such as cramping, mood swings, and heavy or irregular periods. The main active constituents of Salvia miltiorrhiza that contribute to this benefit are tanshinones and salvianolic acids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and hormonal regulatory effects. The primary parts of the Salvia miltiorrhiza plant that provide this benefit are the roots, which contain the highest concentration of the beneficial compounds. The main medicinal preparations of Salvia miltiorrhiza that provide this benefit include dried root extracts, powdered root, and decoctions, which can be found in various forms such as capsules, tablets, and teas. This benefit of Salvia miltiorrhiza is particularly helpful for individuals with menstrual disorders such as dysmenorrhea (painful periods), amenorrhea (absence of menstruation), and premenstrual syndrome (PMS).

Dan Shen, also known as Salvia miltiorrhiza, lessens menstrual pain, meaning it can provide relief for women who experience discomfort during their menstrual period. The main active constituents of Salvia miltiorrhiza that contribute to this benefit are tanshinones and salvianolic acids. The primary parts of the Salvia miltiorrhiza plant that provide this benefit are the roots and rhizomes. The main medicinal preparations of Salvia miltiorrhiza that give this benefit include decoctions, powdered extracts, and capsules. This benefit of Salvia miltiorrhiza is particularly helpful for women who suffer from menstrual pain and discomfort associated with menstrual cramps, bloating, and irritability.

benefits.benefit_name: Promotes blood flow in the pelvic area
benefits.benefit_desc: Dan Shen, also known as Salvia miltiorrhiza, promotes blood flow in the pelvic area, meaning it helps increase the delivery of oxygen and nutrient-rich blood to this region of the body. The main active constituents of Salvia miltiorrhiza that give this benefit are tanshinones and salvianolic acids, which have been shown to have vasodilatory and antiplatelet effects. The primary parts of the Salvia miltiorrhiza plant that give this benefit are the roots, which contain the highest concentration of these active compounds. The main medicinal preparations of Salvia miltiorrhiza that give this benefit include decoctions, powdered extracts, and tinctures. This benefit of Salvia miltiorrhiza is particularly helpful for individuals with health conditions that involve reduced blood flow to the pelvic area, such as chronic pelvic pain, endometriosis, and interstitial cystitis.

Dan Shen, also known as Salvia miltiorrhiza, helps in wound healing, meaning it promotes the restoration of damaged tissue to its normal structure and function. The main active constituents of Salvia miltiorrhiza that contribute to this benefit are tanshinones and salvianolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties. The root of the Salvia miltiorrhiza plant is the primary part used for medicinal purposes, as it contains high levels of these beneficial compounds. The main medicinal preparations of Salvia miltiorrhiza that provide this benefit include decoctions, powder, and ethanol or water extracts. This benefit of Salvia miltiorrhiza is particularly helpful in relieving wounds, cuts, burns, and other skin injuries, as well as inflammatory skin conditions such as acne and eczema.

Dan Shen, also known as Salvia miltiorrhiza, protects liver health by acting as a hepatoprotective agent, meaning it helps to prevent damage to the liver and can even help to regenerate damaged liver cells. The main active constituents of Salvia miltiorrhiza that contribute to this benefit are tanshinones and salvianolic acids, which have been shown to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anti-fibrotic effects on the liver. The primary parts of the Salvia miltiorrhiza plant that provide this benefit are the roots and rhizomes, which contain the highest concentrations of the beneficial compounds. The main medicinal preparations of Salvia miltiorrhiza that provide this benefit are typically in the form of dried root slices, powdered extracts, or capsules, and are often used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat liver diseases such as hepatitis, cirrhosis, and fatty liver disease. Additionally, Salvia miltiorrhiza has been shown to help relieve symptoms associated with liver disease, such as inflammation, oxidative stress, and fibrosis.

Dan Shen, also known as Salvia miltiorrhiza, reduces the risk of atherosclerosis, meaning it helps prevent the buildup of plaque in the arteries that can lead to this serious condition. The main active constituents of Salvia miltiorrhiza that contribute to this benefit are tanshinones and salvianolic acids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-platelet effects. The primary parts of the Salvia miltiorrhiza plant that have this benefit are the roots, which contain the highest concentration of these active compounds. The main medicinal preparations of Salvia miltiorrhiza that give this benefit include dried root extracts, decoctions, and capsules. This benefit of Salvia miltiorrhiza is particularly helpful for individuals with cardiovascular disease, hypertension, and high cholesterol levels, as it may help improve blood flow, reduce inflammation, and prevent the formation of blood clots.

Dan Shen, also known as Salvia miltiorrhiza, lowers blood pressure, meaning it can reduce high blood pressure levels in individuals with hypertension. The main active constituents of Salvia miltiorrhiza that contribute to this benefit are tanshinones and salvianolic acids. The primary parts of the Salvia miltiorrhiza plant that give this benefit are the roots and rhizomes. The main medicinal preparations of Salvia miltiorrhiza that give this benefit include decoctions, powdered extracts, and capsules. The main health conditions that this benefit of Salvia miltiorrhiza helps relieve include hypertension, atherosclerosis, and other cardiovascular diseases.
